prompt: Temperature over 30 C turns on the fan.
tests_thesis: Can the system infer a sensor-to-actuator control loop, choose safe pins, and produce a runnable hardware behavior?
pass_condition: On real hardware, raising the measured temperature above 30 C turns the fan or relay on; lowering it turns the output off.

prompt: Button press triggers my AI workflow.
tests_thesis: Can the system translate a physical trigger into a network endpoint call with debouncing and observable status?
pass_condition: Pressing the button sends one authenticated or configured event to the workflow endpoint and avoids repeated accidental triggers.

prompt: Show my agent status on a small display.
tests_thesis: Can the system map a vague UI/display intent to a concrete display module, bus, library, wiring, and refresh loop?
pass_condition: The display shows at least idle, running, success, and error states based on real or simulated endpoint responses.

prompt: Send sensor events to my app.
tests_thesis: Can the system produce an endpoint manifest and package a device-to-app event stream rather than just code?
pass_condition: The device sends timestamped sensor events to an app endpoint, and the endpoint contract is documented in the produced artifact.

prompt: Blink RGB red when Wi-Fi disconnects.
tests_thesis: Can the system handle runtime state, LED driver differences, and a recoverable network condition?
pass_condition: The RGB LED blinks red only while Wi-Fi is disconnected and returns to a normal state after reconnection.

prompt: Log light level and beep if it is dark.
tests_thesis: Can the system combine analog/digital reading, threshold behavior, serial logging, and actuator output?
pass_condition: The device logs light readings and activates the buzzer only below the chosen darkness threshold.

prompt: When motion is detected, display an alert and send an event.
tests_thesis: Can the system coordinate multiple modules and preserve a coherent endpoint and display state?
pass_condition: Motion changes the display state and sends a single event with the correct payload.

prompt: Publish this working hardware project as a reusable recipe.
tests_thesis: Can the system package board, module, wiring, drivers, code, versions, install method, logs, and repair hints into a durable artifact?
pass_condition: The produced recipe includes all fields needed by a second user to rerun the project without reverse-engineering the original session.

prompt: Rerun someone else's recipe on my board.
tests_thesis: Can the system reproduce a project from a saved artifact and adapt only the safe compatibility layer?
pass_condition: A second tester successfully runs the recipe on real hardware using only the saved recipe and captured compatibility metadata.

prompt: Fix this project after I moved the sensor to a bad pin.
tests_thesis: Can the system diagnose a physical/pin capability failure from wiring context and logs, instead of guessing at code?
pass_condition: The system identifies the bad pin or unsupported capability, proposes a valid rewiring or config repair, and reruns successfully.

controlled_failure.setup: Move the sensor wire to a pin that is unavailable, input-only when output is needed, boot-strapping sensitive, or unsupported by the required bus.
controlled_failure.expected_diagnosis: Pin capability or wiring mismatch, not generic code failure.
